entries.name: accordion
entries.title: Accordion CDK
entries.description: The Accordion CDK provides a set of directives for creating collapsible content sections with smooth animations. It consists of several directives that work together to create expandable/collapsible content areas, supporting both individual collapsible sections and accordion-style behavior where only one section can be open at a time.

sections.title: Usage
sections.description: A simple example of using the axCarousel directive

sections.title: Accordion mode
sections.description: With accordion, you can only have one section open at a time.

sections.title: Without toggle button
sections.description: Without axAccordionCloseButton, you can click on the header to control the collapse state.

entries.name: carousel
entries.title: Carousel Directive
entries.description: Integrates Swiper.js into ACoreX applications to create highly customizable and responsive carousels/sliders. The directive handles the initialization and configuration of the Swiper instance, automatically applying necessary CSS classes to your HTML structure.

sections.title: Usage
sections.description: A simple example of using the axCarousel directive

entries.name: drag-drop
entries.title: Drag and Drop
entries.description: A flexible set of directives for implementing drag-and-drop functionality in your Angular applications. The drag-drop system consists of four main directives: axDrag for draggable elements, axDropList for sortable lists, axDropZone for drop areas, and axDragHandle for custom drag handles.

sections.title: Usage
sections.description: A simple example of using axDrag directive to make an element draggable

sections.title: Axis Lock
sections.description: Use the dragLockAxis input to constrain dragging to either the 'x' (horizontal) or 'y' (vertical) axis

sections.title: Boundary
sections.description: The dragBoundary input restricts movement to within a specified HTML element or a CSS selector

sections.title: Drag Handle
sections.description: Use the axDragHandle directive to specify a child element that acts as the handle for dragging the parent

sections.title: Drop Zone
sections.description: The axDropZone directive creates an area where draggable items can be dropped. It can emit events on hover and drop

sections.title: Clone Drag
sections.description: Set dragElementClone to true to drag a copy of the element, leaving the original in place. A custom dragClonedTemplate can also be provided

sections.title: Drop List
sections.description: The axDropList directive enables reordering of axDrag items within a container. Drag items within the list to sort them

sections.title: Connected Lists
sections.description: You can connect multiple axDropList containers by giving them the same dropListGroup. This allows items to be dragged from one list to another

entries.name: drawer
entries.title: Drawer
entries.description: Drawer directive provides a simple way to integrate an off-canvas panel. To use it, apply axDrawerContainer to the parent and axDrawerItem to the drawer element. It supports properties like mode (overlay or push), transition (animation duration), backdropClass, drawerLocation (start, end, top, or bottom), backDrop (boolean for enabling/disabling the backdrop), and collapsed (boolean for initial state). The directive also includes three methods show(), hide(), and toggle() for programmatic control, making it easy to manage the drawer's behavior dynamically.

sections.title: Usage
sections.description: A simple example of using axDrawerContainer and axDrawerItem

sections.title: Modes
sections.description: Drawer in overlay and push mode

sections.title: Location
sections.description: Drawer in start, end, top, or bottom location

entries.name: focus-trap
entries.title: Focus Trap
entries.description: A directive that traps keyboard focus within a specific DOM element, ensuring that Tab and Shift+Tab navigation remains within the boundaries of the element. This is particularly useful for modal dialogs, drawers, and other UI components that need to maintain focus within their boundaries.

sections.title: Usage
sections.description: A simple example of using axFocusTrap directive

entries.name: input-mask
entries.title: Input Mask
entries.description: A powerful Angular directive that provides input masking functionality for various data types including Hijri dates, digits, decimal numbers, and thousands-separated numbers. Built on top of the Maska.js library for robust input validation and formatting.

entries.name: list-navigation
entries.title: List Navigation
entries.description: A directive that facilitates keyboard navigation within lists. The parent element uses the axListNavigation directive, and each child element uses the axListNavigationItem directive. Supports navigation through list items using Tab, Arrow Down, and Arrow Up keys, with both horizontal and vertical orientations. Features keyboard event handling and focus management for efficient list navigation.

sections.title: Usage
sections.description: Basic usage of the list navigation directive with default settings.

sections.title: Orientation
sections.description: Change the navigation direction using the orientation property.

entries.name: outline
entries.title: Outline
entries.description: The ACoreX outline directives provide a robust and streamlined way to create dynamic, scroll-linked navigation menus within ACoreX applications. These directives simplify the process of generating table of contents or side navigation elements that automatically highlight the currently visible section of a page as the user scrolls, enhancing user experience on long-form content.

sections.title: Usage
sections.description: A complete example showing how to use the outline directives together to create a dynamic navigation system.

sections.title: Hash Links
sections.description: Demonstrates how to enable hash links with the outline directive for better navigation and URL sharing.

sections.description: Shows how to configure the activation offset to control when outline items become active during scrolling.

sections.title: Target Selector
sections.description: Demonstrates how to use CSS selectors to automatically include elements in the outline navigation.

entries.name: pan-view
entries.title: Pan View
entries.description: The AXPanViewDirective provides a way to implement pan and zoom functionality for a designated content area within your application. This directive allows users to interact with content by panning (dragging) it around and zooming in or out, which is useful for scenarios where you have content that might be larger than the viewport or require detailed inspection.

sections.description: Toggle between standard middle-click panning and free left-click panning mode.

sections.title: Zoom Controls
sections.description: Programmatic zoom control using directive methods and zoom change events.

sections.title: Model Properties
sections.description: Two-way binding with panX, panY, and zoom model properties.

entries.name: selection
entries.title: Selection
entries.description: The selection CDK provides directives for creating interactive selection functionality in lists and collections. It supports both single and multiple selection modes, with built-in state management and two-way data binding. The selection system consists of two main directives: axSelectionGroup for the container and axSelectionItem for individual selectable items.

sections.title: Usage
sections.description: A basic example of using the selection directives.

sections.title: Multiple
sections.description: Enable multiple selection using the multiple property.

entries.name: sliding-item
entries.title: Sliding Item
entries.description: A directive that enables horizontal sliding functionality for elements. It provides interactive drag-to-slide behavior with configurable sliding directions and smooth animations. The directive automatically handles pointer events and provides visual feedback during interactions.

sections.title: Usage
sections.description: A simple example of using the axSlidingItem directive

entries.name: uploader
entries.title: Uploader
entries.description: The Uploader CDK provides directives and services for file upload functionality, including drag-and-drop support, file browsing, and upload management. The [axUploaderZone] directive can be applied to any element to enable drag-and-drop file uploads. The [axUploaderBrowseHandle] directive provides click-to-browse functionality when used within an uploader zone.

entries.name: virtual-scroll
entries.title: Virtual Scroll
entries.description: Virtual scrolling directive efficiently renders large datasets by only displaying items visible in the viewport. It requires a height input from the user, defining the scrollable area. As the user scrolls, content is dynamically loaded and unloaded to optimize performance. The directive includes a ScrollEnd event for triggering actions when reaching the end of the list.

sections.title: Usage
sections.description: A basic example of using virtual scroll with large datasets

entries.name: wysiwyg
entries.title: WYSIWYG
entries.description: The AXWysiwyg class is a wrapper around the Quill rich text editor, designed to simplify its usage in ACorex library. This class provides an easy way to initialize a Quill editor within a specified container and apply various formatting options such as bold, italic, underline, strike-through, font color, text alignment, and more. It also includes methods to manipulate text direction, font size, and background color. Users can insert images, and undo/redo changes using the integrated history features of Quill. The HTMLoutput getter provides access to the HTML content of the editor. The class enhances usability by offering a clean API for interacting with the Quill editor within Angular applications.

entries.name: components

entries.name: constants

entries.name: date-time

import { AXDateFormatter, AXDateTimeFormatter, AXTimeFormatter, AXTimeDurationFormatter, AXTimeLeftFormatter } from '@acorex/core/date-time';

entries.name: events
entries.title: Event Service
entries.description: A root-level service for application-wide communication using two complementary patterns: an RxJS Observable stream (emitEvent / onEvent) and a lightweight callback-based event bus (on / broadcast). Includes predefined AXSystemEvents for locale, language, and calendar changes.

entries.name: file
entries.title: File Utilities
entries.description: Browser-side file utilities: programmatic file selection via a hidden input, Blob-to-Base64 conversion, base64 size calculation, and human-readable file size formatting through the format pipe.

entries.name: format
entries.title: Format System
entries.description: A pluggable Angular formatting framework: registry-based formatters, built-in number and string formatters, reactive format pipe, structural directive, fluent API, and extension points for date-time, file size, and custom formatters.
imports: import { AXFormatService, AXFormatModule, AXFormatPipe, AXFormatterDirective, AXFormatter, AXFormatterRegistryService, AXNumberFormatter, AXStringFormatter } from '@acorex/core/format';

entries.name: icon
entries.title: Icon Resolver
entries.description: A pluggable icon resolution system: register custom AXIconResolver implementations via dependency injection, resolve icon keys to SVG strings or URLs through AXIconResolverService, with built-in caching and multi-resolver chaining.
imports: import { AXIconResolverService, AXIconResolver, AX_ICON_RESOLVER, AXIconResolverDefault } from '@acorex/core/icon';

entries.name: image
entries.title: Image Service
entries.description: Browser-side image resizing via canvas: scale images from File or HTMLImageElement sources down to a max dimension while preserving aspect ratio, with configurable output format and quality.

entries.name: locale
entries.title: Locale System
entries.description: Locale profile management for AcoreX: lazy-loaded regional settings (date/time formats, calendar, units, RTL), reactive switching via signals, provider-based extensibility, and deep integration with translation and date-time formatting.
imports: import { AXLocaleService, AXLocaleProfileProviderService, AX_LOCALE_CONFIG, AX_LOCALE_PROFILE_PROVIDERS, AXLocaleProfile, AXLocaleProfileProvider, AXUSLocaleProfile, AXIRLocaleProfile, AXAULocaleProfile } from '@acorex/core/locale';

entries.name: memoize
entries.title: Memoize Decorator
entries.description: A method decorator that caches synchronous method results using memoizee. Repeated calls with the same arguments return cached values; the cache is cleared automatically on ngOnDestroy to prevent memory leaks.

entries.name: network
entries.title: Network Service
entries.description: Browser-aware network utilities: online/offline status signal, file uploads and downloads with progress tracking, cancellation, and typed status events built on Angular HttpClient.
imports: import { AXNetworkService, AXUploadTask, AXDownloadTask, AXUploadStatus, AXDownloadStatus, AXUploadResponse, AXDownloadResponse } from '@acorex/core/network';

entries.name: platform
entries.title: Platform Service
entries.description: Runtime environment detection and application chrome utilities: device/browser/screen checks, dark/light theme management with persistence, global resize/click/scroll/direction events, and SSR-safe isBrowser() helpers.

entries.name: rrule-service

entries.description: Service for working with RRule recurrence patterns. Provides methods to create RRule instances from options or strings, convert between formats, and query occurrences.

entries.name: pipes

entries.description: Angular pipe that wraps DomSanitizer to mark trusted HTML, styles, scripts, URLs, and resource URLs as safe for template binding — used throughout AcoreX documentation and demo components.

entries.name: translation

entries.name: font-icons
entries.title: Font icons
entries.description: Let us first explain how icons work in ACoreX. In ACoreX, instead of creating a dedicated icon font, we decided to leverage existing icon fonts. In the components section, we initially needed a set of icons, so we started by using a few fixed icons. However, since each project might require different icons, mixing a project’s own icons with the default icons used in ACoreX could create a sense of inconsistency and lack of visual harmony. To address this, we created a map file for each available icon package. This means that if you are using Material icons in your project and load the corresponding Material map file, ACoreX will replace its fixed icons in components with Material icons. For example, if we used the chevron-right icon in a component, under the hood it would reference the icon’s class or code point "e5cc". This way, you can not only use the three packages prepared by default (Font Awesome, Huge Icons, and Material Icons), but you can also follow the same pattern to integrate any custom icon font of your choice.

entries.name: installation
entries.title: Installation
entries.description: To install and use ACoreX, you must complete the following steps in full and then move on to the next step.

entries.name: introduction
entries.title: Introduction
entries.description: ACoreX is a modern, and fully responsive UI library designed to help developers build beautiful and functional web applications quickly and efficiently. It is built with Tailwind CSS and TypeScript, and provides a comprehensive set of components and utilities that can be easily customized to fit your project's needs.

entries.name: tailwind-compatibility
entries.title: Tailwind compatibility
entries.description: To use Tailwind with ACoreX, you need to integrate them. We’ve provided functions and classes that make development easier and faster for you.

entries.name: colors
entries.title: Colors
entries.description: In ACoreX, we have five primary colors — primary, secondary, success, warning, and danger — as well as seven accent colors, which are not defined by default but can be configured as needed.Each color has seven corresponding surface shades, which are used to define the colors of components and the overall application.Additionally, to ensure compatibility with TailwindCSS, the colors also include shades ranging from 50 to 950.

entries.name: sizing
entries.title: Sizing
entries.description: Components and elements in ACoreX support sizing options, with size values including ax-xs, ax-sm, ax-md, ax-lg, and ax-xl.These values are defined as CSS variables and applied through a simple formula that affects the height or dimensions of a component or element.

entries.name: tailwind-generator
entries.title: Tailwind Generator
entries.description: If you are using Tailwind, you need to generate Tailwind classes for the colors you add. By default, we generate primary, secondary, success, warning, danger and surface colors. for example, you added a color to the theme called Accent 1 and needed it in Tailwind, you can generate classes with the functions provided to you.

entries.name: theming
entries.title: Theming
entries.description: Using the SCSS structure below, you can customize your application’s theme or generate its output into CSS files, allowing you to create multiple themes for your application.

entries.name: typography
entries.title: Typography
entries.description: Typography in ACoreX is very straightforward and follows web standards. To add a font family, simply use the standard method.Additionally, we’ve provided a set of ready-to-use classes that you can apply to headings and subheadings.

entries.description: Type definition for the visual appearance style of the bottom navigation component. This type defines the different look variants available for styling the bottom navigation bar.

entries.name: ax-calendar-cell-css-class
entries.title: Calendar Cell CSS Class
entries.description: Defines the type for specifying CSS classes for calendar cells, allowing custom styling of individual date cells.

entries.name: ax-calendar-disabled-dates
entries.title: Calendar Disabled Dates
entries.description: Defines the type for specifying dates that should be disabled in calendar components, preventing user selection of those dates.

entries.name: ax-calendar-holiday-dates
entries.title: Calendar Holiday Dates
entries.description: Defines the type for specifying holiday dates in calendar components, allowing special styling and behavior for these dates.

entries.name: ax-calendar-view-depth
entries.title: Calendar View Depth
entries.description: Defines the depth level for calendar view selection, determining the granularity of date selection and navigation.

entries.name: ax-calendar-view-type
entries.title: Calendar View Type
entries.description: Defines the different view types available for calendar components, specifying how dates are displayed and organized.

entries.name: ax-circular-progress-mode
entries.title: Circular Progress Mode
entries.description: Type definition for the circular progress mode.

entries.name: menu-item
entries.title: Command Menu Item
entries.description: Interface defining the structure of a command menu item used in command components and other menu-related components.

entries.name: component-state
entries.title: Component State
entries.description: Defines the possible states for AX components, indicating their current validation or status condition.

entries.name: ax-conversation-message
entries.title: Conversation Message
entries.description: Represents a message in a conversation with properties for identification, content, timing, and user information. This interface defines the structure for individual messages within a conversation component.

entries.name: ax-data-pager-data
entries.title: Data Pager Data
entries.description: Represents the data structure containing pagination information including page details, range, and pagination metadata.

entries.name: ax-data-pager-mode
entries.title: Data Pager Mode
entries.description: Defines the display mode options for the data pager component, controlling how pagination controls are presented to the user.

entries.name: ax-dialog-button-item
entries.title: Dialog Button Item
entries.description: Interface defining the properties of a button item within a dialog. This interface allows customization of button appearance, behavior, and interaction handling.

entries.name: ax-dialog-config
entries.title: Dialog Configuration
entries.description: Configuration interface for dialog appearance and behavior. This interface defines all the properties that can be used to customize the dialog component, including title, content, buttons, and interaction options.

entries.name: ax-dialog-data
entries.title: Dialog Data
entries.description: Interface defining the data structure passed to dialog components. This interface represents the internal data format used by the dialog component to render its content and configuration.

entries.name: ax-dialog-ref
entries.title: Dialog Reference
entries.description: Interface providing programmatic control over a dialog instance. This interface allows developers to close dialogs programmatically and manage dialog lifecycle.

entries.name: ax-direction
entries.title: Direction Type
entries.description: A type that specifies the direction of component layout.

entries.name: ax-download-response
entries.title: Download Response
entries.description: Represents the payload emitted during a download operation, including progress state, byte counts, status, and the Blob data on completion.

entries.name: ax-drawer-location
entries.title: Drawer Location
entries.description: Type definition for the location where a drawer appears on the screen. Supports RTL-aware positioning.

entries.name: ax-drawer-mode
entries.title: Drawer Mode
entries.description: Type definition for the display mode of a drawer. Determines how the drawer appears relative to the main content.

entries.name: editor-js-tool
entries.title: EditorJsTool
entries.description: Configuration object describing an EditorJS tool that can be enabled in the editor.

entries.name: ax-flow-chart-edges
entries.title: Flow Chart Edges
entries.description: Represents a connection between two nodes in the flow chart, including identifiers and endpoint anchors.

entries.name: ax-flow-chart-nodes
entries.title: Flow Chart Nodes
entries.description: Represents a node in the flow chart with identity, type, label, and position.

entries.name: ax-form-message-style
entries.title: Form Message Style
entries.description: Type definition for the message display style in form fields. Determines how validation and error messages are positioned relative to form inputs.

entries.name: ax-form-update-on
entries.title: Form Update On
entries.description: Type definition for when form validation and updates should be triggered. Determines the timing of form field validation and value updates.

entries.name: form-validation-rule
entries.title: Form Validation Rule
entries.description: Represents a validation rule applied to a form field, including its type, status, and options.

entries.name: ax-grid-layout-node
entries.title: Grid Layout Node
entries.description: Represents a complete grid layout node that extends the widget interface with additional element reference. This interface combines position, widget properties, and DOM element reference for comprehensive grid item management.

entries.name: ax-grid-layout-options
entries.title: Grid Layout Options
entries.description: Configuration options for the grid layout system. This interface defines all the customizable properties that control the behavior, appearance, and functionality of the grid layout container.

entries.name: ax-hot-key-action
entries.title: Hot Key Action
entries.description: Type definition for hotkey action configuration. This type allows mapping keyboard shortcuts to specific actions or functions, enabling keyboard-driven interactions in components.

entries.name: ax-input-mask-modes
entries.title: Input Mask Modes
entries.description: Defines the available input mask modes for different types of data formatting and validation.

entries.name: ax-kanban-item
entries.title: Kanban Item
entries.description: Type for items rendered in the Kanban component.

entries.name: ax-kanban-status
entries.title: Kanban Status
entries.description: Type defining a Kanban status (column).

entries.name: ax-label-mode
entries.title: Label Mode
entries.description: Type definition for the label display mode in form fields. Determines how labels are positioned and displayed relative to form inputs.

entries.name: line-chart-data
entries.title: Line Chart Data
entries.description: Interface definition for line chart data series configuration

entries.name: line-chart-messages
entries.title: Line Chart Messages
entries.description: Interface for customizable messages displayed in line chart empty states

entries.name: line-chart-option
entries.title: Line Chart Option
entries.description: Configuration interface for line chart component with comprehensive styling and behavior options

entries.name: line-chart-value
entries.title: Line Chart Value
entries.description: Type definition for line chart data - supports both single series and array of series

entries.name: outline-item-type
entries.title: Outline Item Type
entries.description: Type definition for outline items used by the ACoreX outline navigation system. This type represents the structure of individual navigation items within an outline container.

entries.name: ax-scheduler-appointment
entries.title: Scheduler Appointment
entries.description: Defines the structure of an appointment in the scheduler component.

entries.name: ax-scheduler-appointment-data-source
entries.title: Scheduler Appointment Data Source
entries.description: Defines the data source types for scheduler appointments.

entries.name: ax-scheduler-appointment-loader
entries.title: Scheduler Appointment Loader
entries.description: Defines the synchronous loader function type for scheduler appointments.

entries.name: ax-scheduler-appointment-loader-async
entries.title: Scheduler Appointment Loader Async
entries.description: Defines the asynchronous loader function type for scheduler appointments.

entries.name: ax-scheduler-resource
entries.title: Scheduler Resource
entries.description: Defines the structure of a resource in the scheduler component.

entries.name: ax-scheduler-view
entries.title: Scheduler View Type
entries.description: Defines the available view modes for the scheduler component.

entries.name: ax-selection-group-selected-keys
entries.title: Selection Group Selected Keys
entries.description: Type definition for the selected keys in a selection group. This type represents either a single selected key or an array of selected keys, depending on whether multiple selection is enabled.

entries.name: ax-selection-list-look
entries.title: Selection List Look Type
entries.description: A type that specifies the visual appearance style of the selection list component.

entries.name: selection-mode
entries.title: Selection Mode
entries.description: Type that defines how items can be selected within a component: single, multiple, or none.

entries.name: ax-side-menu-item
entries.title: Side Menu Item
entries.description: Interface defining the structure of a side menu item with support for nested children, routing, and various states.

entries.name: ax-side-menu-location
entries.title: Side Menu Location Type
entries.description: Type definition for the indicator location in side menu components with line-based looks.

entries.name: ax-side-menu-look
entries.title: Side Menu Look Type
entries.description: Type definition for the visual appearance style of the side menu component.

entries.name: time-duration-unit
entries.title: Time Duration Unit
entries.description: Defines the available time units for the time duration component.

entries.name: ax-upload-response
entries.title: Upload Response
entries.description: Represents the payload emitted during an upload operation, including progress state, byte counts, status, and optional result and message.
